title: Today's Tech Brief: Crypto-Funded Chinese Peptide Labs Are Booming
summary: A look at the latest tech news, including crypto-funded Chinese peptide labs, Meta's face recognition code, and more.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. I've got some interesting stories to share with you, straight from the world of technology. Let's dive in.

First up, crypto-funded Chinese peptide labs are booming. According to a report from WIRED, these labs are receiving funding from cryptocurrency sources, and it's raising some eyebrows. Peptide labs are research facilities that focus on creating and studying peptides, which are short chains of amino acids. The use of cryptocurrency funding is interesting, as it suggests that these labs may be operating outside of traditional funding channels. This trend could have significant implications for the field of biotechnology and our understanding of peptides.

In other news, Meta has been quietly stashing dormant face recognition code on more than 50 million phones. This code is part of the companion app that pairs with Meta's Ray-Ban and Oakley smart glasses. If activated, this code could potentially allow Meta to identify users based on their facial features. It's an interesting move, and one that raises questions about user privacy and consent. I'm curious to see how users react to this news, and whether Meta will face any backlash.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.

sources.title: Crypto-Funded Chinese Peptide Labs Are Booming
